pattern: Sorting then pairing the smallest remaining with the largest remaining minimizes the largest pair sum, a classic greedy exchange argument.
hints: If you pair a large number with another large number, that pair sum becomes hard to beat.
Sort the values, then think about pairing extremes together so big numbers are balanced by small ones.
Pair index i with index n-1-i after sorting and take the maximum of those pair sums.

edge_inputs.description: all equal so every pair sum identical

stress.description: random even-length arrays over the full value range
